segments.text: Job replied,
Please listen carefully to what I say—that would be one comfort you could give me. [^127]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Bear with me; let me speak. After I've spoken you can resume mocking me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Am I complaining against people? Of course not. [^128] Why shouldn't I be impatient?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Just take a look at me. Aren't you appalled? Cover your mouth with your hand in shock!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Every time I think of what's happened to me I am horrified and I shake all over with fear.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do the wicked continue to live, to grow old and increasingly powerful?[^129]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Their children are with them; they watch their grandchildren grow up.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They live in their homes in safety—they are not afraid. God does not use his rod to beat them. [^130]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Their bulls always breed successfully; their cows give birth to calves and do not miscarry.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They send out their little ones like lambs to play; their children dance around.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They sing accompanied by the tambourine and lyre; they celebrate with the music of the flute.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They live out their lives contentedly, and go down to Sheol in peace. [^131]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet they tell God, ‘Get lost! We don't want anything to do with you.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Who does the Almighty think he is for us to serve him as slaves? What benefit is there for us if we pray to him?’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Such people believe they make their own fortune, but I don't accept their way of thinking. [^132]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How often is the lamp of the wicked snuffed out?[^133] How often does disaster come upon them?[^134] How often does God punish the wicked in his anger?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Are they blown along like straw in the wind? Does a tornado come in and carry them away?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Some say, ‘God saves up people's punishment for their children.’ But I say, ‘God should punish those people themselves so that they can learn from it.’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Let them see their destruction themselves, and drink deeply from God's anger.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For they don't care what happens to their families once they're dead.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Can anyone teach God anything he doesn't already know, since he is the one who judges even heavenly beings?[^135]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: One person dies in good health, totally comfortable and secure.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Their body is fat from eating well; their bones still strong. [^136]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Another dies after a miserable life without ever experiencing happiness.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Yet they are both buried in the same dust; they are treated alike in death, eaten by maggots.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Look, I know what you're thinking, and your schemes to do me wrong.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You may ask me, ‘Where is the home of the great man? Where is the place where the wicked live?’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Haven't you asked people who travel? Don't you pay attention to what they tell you?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Wicked people are spared in times of disaster; they are rescued from the day of judgment.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Who confronts them with their actions? Who pays them back for what they have done?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When they eventually die and are carried to the graveyard, their tomb is guarded. The earth of the grave softly covers them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Everyone attends their funeral service; a huge procession of people comes to pay their last respects. [^137]
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do you try to comfort me with worthless nonsense?[^138] Your answers are just a pack of lies!
segments.speaker: Job
